rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical dehydrogenative annulation between N-vinylamides (enamides) and O-nucleophiles (water, alcohols, carboxylic acids) to form 4-oxo-oxazolines, mediated by anodically generated bromine from NaBr.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of Br- to Br2 (mediator); Br2 functionalizes the enamide N-H to an N-Br intermediate which homolyzes to an N-centered radical; a second anodic SET converts the resulting C-radical to a carbocation. Cathode reduces H2O to H2/OH-.

key_intermediates.name: O-nucleophile adduct (J, e.g., hydroxyethyl-amide from H2O addition)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes Br- to Br2, which brominates the amide N-H of the enamide to give N-Br species A; homolysis gives amidyl radical B that tautomerizes to alpha-C radical C; a second anodic one-electron oxidation produces N-acyliminium/carbocation D, which undergoes O-nucleophile addition, intramolecular cyclization, and deprotonation to the 4-oxo-oxazoline. Cathode reduces water to H2 and hydroxide.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction partially inhibited (2a yield drops to 30%); TEMPO-radical adduct E (C18H27N2O2+, m/z 303.2056) and its hydrolysates F (C7H8NO+, 122.0599) and G (C11H22NO2+, 200.1639) detected by HRMS, confirming carbon radical C.

probe_reagents.name: H2O (nucleophile trap on cation)
probe_reagents.observation: Open-chain hydroxyethyl amide H (C9H10NO2+, 164.0701) and hydrolysate I (C2H4O2+, 61.0283) detected by HRMS, supporting cationic intermediate D.

probe_reagents.name: NaBr / 1a CV
probe_reagents.observation: NaBr oxidation peak at 1.19 V; 1a shows no oxidation between 0–2 V, indicating reaction is initiated by anodic oxidation of bromide rather than direct substrate oxidation.

probe_reagents.name: benzamide (observed byproduct)
probe_reagents.observation: Trace benzamide observed as side product, consistent with hydrolysis of intermediates C/D.

notes: Bromide serves as a redox mediator (in situ Br2 generation) rather than as an incorporated atom. The mechanism proceeds through a radical (C) → cation (D) sequence (radical-polar crossover). Authors note intermediate D is supported because nucleophilic water adduct H was also detected. Cathode half-reaction is H2O reduction (H2 + OH-).
